queries: Project Name, Project Number, and Project Location.
Owner name, address, and contact information.
General Contractor or Construction Manager name and address.
Subcontractor name, Subcontractor address, and contact information.
Date of Agreement, Effective Date, and Execution Date.
Signatures, titles, and execution blocks for the parties.
Total Contract Sum, Contract Price, or Subcontract Amount.
Schedule of Values, Unit Prices, and breakdown of costs.
Retainage percentage, retained amount, and conditions for release of retainage.
Warranty period, guarantee duration, and correction of defective work.
Standard one-year warranty and obligations to repair.
Scope of Work, specific materials to furnish, and labor to install.
Exclusions from the Subcontractor's scope of work.
Items furnished by Owner or Contractor (Provided by Others).
Specific tasks, duties, and responsibilities of the Subcontractor.
Reference to architectural plans, specifications, drawings, and Exhibit scope documents.
Alternates, allowances, and approved substitutions.
Project schedule, commencement date, and estimated start date.
Milestones, sequencing, phasing, and Substantial Completion date.
Time is of the essence clause.
Procedures for claiming an extension of time or excusable delay.
No damages for delay limitations.
Liquidated damages, financial penalties for delay, and acceleration of work.
Pay-if-paid, pay-when-paid, and condition precedent to payment clauses.
Application for Payment, invoicing procedures, and billing deadlines.
Payment terms, net days for payment after receipt of funds.
Conditional and unconditional lien waivers and release of claims.
Contractor's right to withhold payment, backcharges, and offset rights.
Payment requirements for materials stored off-site.
Subcontractor obligation to indemnify, defend, and hold harmless the Contractor and Owner.
Broad form indemnity and action over employee injury claims.
Limitations on liability, sole negligence, and anti-indemnity acts.
Commercial General Liability (CGL) insurance requirements and policy limits.
Workers Compensation and Employers Liability insurance limits.
Umbrella liability, excess liability, and automobile insurance limits.
Requirement to name Contractor and Owner as Additional Insureds.
Documents incorporated by reference, including the Prime Contract and General Conditions.
Flow-down provisions, assumption of Owner obligations by Subcontractor.
List of attached exhibits, schedules, and addenda.
Order of precedence among contract documents.
Contractor's right to Termination for Convenience.
Termination for Cause and Subcontractor default.
Notice of default, written notice, and the required cure period.
Dispute resolution process, mediation, and binding arbitration.
Litigation, designated venue, jurisdiction, and governing law.
Davis-Bacon Act, prevailing wage requirements, and HUD mandates.
Certified payroll submission and labor compliance reporting.
Jobsite safety rules, OSHA compliance, and safety manuals.
Mandatory reporting requirements for accidents or environmental issues.
E-Verify, equal employment opportunity, and minority business enterprise (MBE) requirements.
